name: Alamo City Credit Union
description_short: Texas-based credit union offering savings accounts, certificates of deposit, and loans at competitive rates to members. Non-profit cooperative returning profits to members via dividends and low loan rates.
description_long:
Alamo City Credit Union is a non-profit cooperative financial institution organized and incorporated under Texas law. As a credit union, it operates as a member-owned association rather than a traditional bank, with a stated mission to help members save money safely and conveniently while providing access to loans at reasonable rates. The organization's structure means all profits generated are returned to members through dividends on savings or reduced rates on loan products.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial services including share savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with rates up to 3.32% APY, signature loans from 9.25% APR, vehicle loans as low as 4.99% APR, and lines of credit. Members can access accounts through in-person services at the branch, online banking via Member.Net, remote deposit capture, and an audio teller phone line. The institution also provides free notary services for members and access to Co-op ATM networks with no ATM fees when using their card.

Alamo City Credit Union differentiates itself through its non-profit member-cooperative structure, which theoretically allows it to offer more competitive rates than traditional banks since profits return to members. The credit union participates in the CO-OP ATM network, providing fee-free access to thousands of ATMs. They also offer specialized products like Special Certificates of Deposit for funds transferred from other institutions, available for limited time periods.

As a small, regional credit union, Alamo City serves a specific membership base in the San Antonio area. While the website demonstrates basic digital banking capabilities (Member.Net, remote deposit, online loan applications), the organization's size means it may have more limited product selection and technology features compared to larger national banks or credit unions. The credit union requires membership eligibility, which may restrict access for some consumers.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Non-profit structure returns all profits to members via dividends and low loan rates
Competitive CD rates up to 3.32% APY as of February 2026
Vehicle loan rates as low as 4.99% APR
Access to nationwide Co-op ATM network with no ATM fees
Multiple account types including share savings, checking (share draft), and certificates of deposit
Online banking through Member.Net and remote deposit capture available
cons: Requires membership eligibility, which restricts access to only qualified consumers
Limited geographic presence as a small regional Texas credit union
Website provides minimal detail on membership requirements or eligibility criteria
Signature loan rates start at 9.25% APR, which is relatively high compared to some competitors
No information provided about product features, limits, or terms on the public website
best_for: San Antonio-area residents eligible for credit union membership seeking competitive savings and loan rates
Members wanting fee-free ATM access through the CO-OP nationwide network
Consumers prioritizing non-profit institution structure and member-owned cooperative benefits
diagnosis: Alamo City Credit Union is best for San Antonio-area residents who meet membership eligibility requirements and want competitive savings rates and loan products from a member-owned, non-profit institution. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is required and not clearly defined on their website, and as a small regional credit union, they lack the product breadth and technological sophistication of larger national competitors.
services: Certificates of deposit with tiered rates
Special certificates of deposit for transferred funds
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
